description: Core governance fields and types: Article, Role, and Decision, with shared fields reusable across all four governance types.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Short human-readable name for the record.

aiGuidance.purpose: A brief, distinct title that identifies this record.
aiGuidance.extraction: Extract the shortest phrase that unambiguously names this record. Prefer the speaker's own words.

fields.name: decision_statement
fields.description: What was decided.

aiGuidance.purpose: The settled commitment or direction of action, stated clearly enough that a non-participant could act on it.
aiGuidance.extraction: State only what was decided. Use plain language, present tense preferred. A decision statement should be complete without the rationale.
aiGuidance.negativeGuidance: Do not include rationale, alternatives, or context. Do not hedge or qualify.

examples.input: We agreed we'd go with the permanent fixings — six millimetre wall plugs, drilled once and left in.
examples.output: Permanent 6mm wall plugs with screws, drilled once and left in place.

fields.name: context
fields.description: Background that makes the matter meaningful.

aiGuidance.purpose: Capture the relevant background, tension, or situation that led to this record.
aiGuidance.extraction: Summarise only the context required to understand the governance issue. Do not include the decision itself unless it is needed to explain the background.
aiGuidance.negativeGuidance: Do not repeat the decision statement here.

fields.name: rationale
fields.description: Reasoning behind the decision.

aiGuidance.purpose: Capture why this decision was chosen — the reasons, constraints, values, and trade-offs that made it the right call.
aiGuidance.extraction: Extract the reasons, forces, values, constraints, and trade-offs that support the decision.
aiGuidance.negativeGuidance: Do not invent reasons that were not present in the source material.

fields.name: alternatives_considered
fields.description: Options that were considered but not chosen.

aiGuidance.purpose: Preserve the meaningful alternatives, rejected options, or counter-proposals that informed the decision.
aiGuidance.extraction: List alternatives that were seriously considered, including why they were not chosen where available.
aiGuidance.negativeGuidance: Do not include options that were never seriously in play.

fields.name: revisit_when
fields.description: Conditions, dates, or triggers that should cause the record to be reviewed.

aiGuidance.purpose: Capture when this record should be revisited, reviewed, or challenged.
aiGuidance.extraction: Extract explicit review dates, triggers, expiry conditions, or evidence that would change the decision.
aiGuidance.negativeGuidance: Do not invent review conditions. If none were stated, leave empty.

fields.name: owner
fields.description: Person, role, group, or function responsible for stewardship of this record.

aiGuidance.purpose: Identify who is responsible for this decision or its follow-through.
aiGuidance.extraction: Extract the name or role of the person or group accountable.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Current governance status of the record.

aiGuidance.purpose: Reflect the current state of the record in the governance lifecycle.
aiGuidance.extraction: Infer status from the source material. Default to 'draft' if not explicitly stated.

fields.name: friction
fields.description: The specific pain, conflict, or gap that makes a decision necessary now.

aiGuidance.purpose: Capture what is not working and why acting matters. Distinct from background context — friction is the pressure, not the history.
aiGuidance.extraction: Extract the specific gap between current state and desired state, or the conflict or issue forcing a choice. Do not conflate with background or context.
aiGuidance.negativeGuidance: Do not include history, constraints, or background here. Those belong in context.

fields.name: decision_question
fields.description: The single concrete question this decision answers, in a form that admits different answers.

aiGuidance.purpose: Make the exact choice being made explicit and testable. A good decision question can be answered in more than one way.
aiGuidance.extraction: Extract or synthesise the specific question. Prefer 'Should we… or…?' form. If the source only states a conclusion, reconstruct the question that conclusion answers.
aiGuidance.negativeGuidance: Do not phrase as a task or action item.

fields.name: key_requirements
fields.description: The standards, values, constraints, and trade-offs used to evaluate options. Includes non-negotiables and recorded disagreements.

aiGuidance.purpose: Record what a good decision looks like for this problem — not just criteria but the values applied and the trade-offs accepted.
aiGuidance.extraction: Extract constraints, values applied, trade-offs accepted, and significant points of disagreement among participants.
aiGuidance.negativeGuidance: Do not repeat the decision itself here. Focus on the standards used to reach it.

fields.name: next_steps
fields.description: Actions or tasks that emerge from the decision. May be empty if the decision itself is the action.

aiGuidance.purpose: Capture what still needs to happen after the decision is made.
aiGuidance.extraction: List named actions with owners where stated. If none, leave empty.
aiGuidance.negativeGuidance: Do not restate the decision. Only record what needs to happen as a result of it.

fields.name: article_text
fields.description: The operative text of the article — what the article actually says.

aiGuidance.purpose: Capture the durable, authoritative wording of the governance rule or commitment.
aiGuidance.negativeGuidance: Do not summarise or paraphrase. Article text is the thing itself, not a description of it.

fields.name: article_number
fields.description: Stable local reference identifier for the article, e.g. A-001.

aiGuidance.purpose: Provide a short, stable, human-readable reference that survives document re-ordering.

fields.name: amendment_rule
fields.description: The process or conditions under which this article may be amended or repealed.

aiGuidance.purpose: Make the constraints on changing this article explicit, so future amendments follow the agreed process.

fields.name: protected_status
fields.description: Whether this article is protected from ordinary amendment by the group.

aiGuidance.purpose: Flag articles that require external consent (e.g. Party Utility agreement) to amend, beyond the normal group process.

fields.name: role_holder
fields.description: The person, team, group, or function currently holding this role.

aiGuidance.purpose: Name who holds this authority boundary at the time of writing. Roles may be unoccupied or transferred.

fields.name: authority
fields.description: What this role may decide or act on without requiring group agreement.

aiGuidance.purpose: Define the scope of unilateral authority. What is this role holder empowered to decide alone?
aiGuidance.extraction: Extract the specific decisions, actions, or domains this role holder can act on independently.
aiGuidance.negativeGuidance: Do not conflate with boundary (what the role cannot do). Authority is what they can do.

fields.name: boundary
fields.description: The limits of this role — where the role's authority ends or must be shared.

aiGuidance.purpose: Define what the role holder cannot decide alone, and where authority overlaps with or defers to others.
aiGuidance.negativeGuidance: Do not restate the authority here. Boundary is what the role cannot do unilaterally.

fields.name: source_of_authority
fields.description: How this authority was conferred: inherited, delegated, elected, appointed, constitutional, etc.

aiGuidance.purpose: Make the legitimacy of the role visible. Where does this authority come from?

types.name: decision
types.description: A settled commitment, choice, policy, or direction of action. Use this Type when a group has agreed on something and needs a durable record of what was decided, why, and when it should be revisited. If the matter is still open, use an Exercise instead.

aiGuidance.purpose: Capture a governance decision — what was decided, why, what was not chosen, and when it should be revisited.
aiGuidance.extraction: Extract from meeting transcripts or deliberation records. A decision must be settled — not a proposal or ongoing discussion. Each decision should be one commitment; split compound decisions.

types.name: article
types.description: A durable governance rule, boundary, constitutional clause, or standing commitment. Articles are written by the clerk after the founding discussion; they are not AI-extracted from transcripts.

aiGuidance.purpose: Capture what the group has constitutionally agreed — the rules, boundaries, and commitments that frame all other governance records.
aiGuidance.negativeGuidance: Articles are not decisions. They are the constitutional layer that makes decisions possible. Do not use an Article to record a single meeting outcome.

types.name: role
types.description: An authority boundary. Defines who may decide or act on what, without requiring group agreement. Roles are written by the clerk; they are not extracted from transcripts.

aiGuidance.purpose: Capture a named authority boundary — who holds it, what they may decide alone, and where their authority ends.
aiGuidance.negativeGuidance: A Role is not a job description. It defines decision rights, not responsibilities or duties.

documentViews.name: foundation-document
documentViews.description: Assembles the LiMoMa constitutional layer (Articles), authority boundaries (Roles), settled commitments (Decisions), and unresolved questions (Exercises) into a single readable foundation document.
documentViews.preamble:
# {{container-title}}

Founded May 2026. This document is the living record of LiMoMa — its purpose, its people, its decisions, and its unresolved questions.

sections.title: Articles
sections.description: Constitutional rules and standing commitments. These define what LiMoMa is and how it operates.

sections.title: Roles
sections.description: Authority boundaries — who may decide what without requiring group agreement.

sections.title: Decision Log
sections.description: Settled commitments, in the order they were made.

sections.title: Exercise Book
sections.description: Open and deferred questions — matters the group has not yet resolved.
